dhikirs.arabicText: حَسْبُنَا اللَّهُ وَنِعْمَ الْوَكِيلُ
dhikirs.transliteration: Hasbunallahu wa ni'mal wakeel
dhikirs.englishTranslation: Sufficient for us is Allah, and He is the best Disposer of affairs
dhikirs.benefit: Said by Prophet Ibrahim (AS) when thrown into fire, and by the Companions before the Battle of Badr. Brings immense peace during anxiety and fear.

dhikirs.arabicText: لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا أَنتَ سُبْحَانَكَ إِنِّي كُنتُ مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ
dhikirs.transliteration: La ilaha illa anta subhanaka inni kuntu minaz-zalimin
dhikirs.englishTranslation: There is no deity except You; exalted are You. Indeed, I have been of the wrongdoers
dhikirs.benefit: The supplication of Prophet Yunus (AS) in the belly of the whale. Allah says no Muslim will call upon Him with this except that He will respond.

dhikirs.arabicText: أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ الْعَظِيمَ الَّذِي لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ الْحَيُّ الْقَيُّومُ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْهِ
dhikirs.transliteration: Astaghfirullaha al-'Azeem alladhi la ilaha illa huwa al-Hayy al-Qayyum wa atubu ilayh
dhikirs.englishTranslation: I seek forgiveness from Allah the Almighty, there is no deity except Him, the Ever-Living, the Sustainer, and I repent to Him
dhikirs.benefit: The Prophet (SAW) said whoever says this, Allah will forgive him even if he fled from battle.

dhikirs.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْهَمِّ وَالْحَزَنِ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْعَجْزِ وَالْكَسَلِ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْجُبْنِ وَالْبُخْلِ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ غَلَبَةِ الدَّيْنِ وَقَهْرِ الرِّجَالِ
dhikirs.transliteration: Allahumma inni a'udhu bika minal-hammi wal-hazan, wa a'udhu bika minal-'ajzi wal-kasal, wa a'udhu bika minal-jubni wal-bukhl, wa a'udhu bika min ghalabatid-dayni wa qahrir-rijal
dhikirs.englishTranslation: O Allah, I seek refuge in You from worry and grief, from weakness and laziness, from cowardice and miserliness, and from being overcome by debt and overpowered by men
dhikirs.benefit: The Prophet (SAW) used to frequently make this comprehensive dua seeking refuge from worry, sadness, and all forms of distress.

dhikirs.arabicText: إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ رَاجِعُونَ، اللَّهُمَّ أْجُرْنِي فِي مُصِيبَتِي وَأَخْلِفْ لِي خَيْرًا مِنْهَا
dhikirs.transliteration: Inna lillahi wa inna ilayhi raji'un. Allahumma'jurni fi musibati wa akhlif li khayran minha
dhikirs.englishTranslation: Indeed we belong to Allah and to Him we shall return. O Allah, reward me in my calamity and replace it with something better
dhikirs.benefit: Umm Salamah (RA) said this when her husband died, and Allah replaced him with the Prophet (SAW) as her husband.

dhikirs.arabicText: أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ الرَّجِيمِ
dhikirs.transliteration: A'udhu billahi minash-shaytanir-rajim
dhikirs.englishTranslation: I seek refuge in Allah from Satan, the accursed
dhikirs.benefit: The Prophet (SAW) advised saying this when angry. Seeking refuge from Shaytan helps calm the heart and dispel anger.

dhikirs.arabicText: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي بِنِعْمَتِهِ تَتِمُّ الصَّالِحَاتُ
dhikirs.transliteration: Alhamdulillahil-ladhi bi ni'matihi tatimmus-salihat
dhikirs.englishTranslation: All praise is due to Allah, by Whose grace good deeds are completed
dhikirs.benefit: Said when something good happens. The Prophet (SAW) taught this expression of gratitude for blessings.

dhikirs.arabicText: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ عَلَى كُلِّ حَالٍ
dhikirs.transliteration: Alhamdulillahi 'ala kulli hal
dhikirs.englishTranslation: All praise is due to Allah in every circumstance
dhikirs.benefit: Said when faced with something disliked. Teaches us to be grateful in all circumstances.

dhikirs.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ لَا سَهْلَ إِلَّا مَا جَعَلْتَهُ سَهْلًا، وَأَنْتَ تَجْعَلُ الْحَزْنَ إِذَا شِئْتَ سَهْلًا
dhikirs.transliteration: Allahumma la sahla illa ma ja'altahu sahla, wa anta taj'alul-hazna idha shi'ta sahla
dhikirs.englishTranslation: O Allah, there is no ease except what You make easy, and You make the difficulty easy if You wish
dhikirs.benefit: A powerful dua for when facing any difficulty. Reminds us that only Allah can make things easy.

dhikirs.arabicText: يَا حَيُّ يَا قَيُّومُ بِرَحْمَتِكَ أَسْتَغِيثُ
dhikirs.transliteration: Ya Hayyu ya Qayyumu bi rahmatika astaghith
dhikirs.englishTranslation: O Ever-Living, O Sustainer, in Your mercy I seek relief
dhikirs.benefit: The Prophet (SAW) taught this dua for times of distress. Calling upon Allah by His beautiful names.

dhikirs.arabicText: رَبِّ اشْرَحْ لِي صَدْرِي وَيَسِّرْ لِي أَمْرِي
dhikirs.transliteration: Rabbish-rahli sadri wa yassirli amri
dhikirs.englishTranslation: My Lord, expand for me my chest and ease for me my task
dhikirs.benefit: The dua of Prophet Musa (AS) before speaking to Pharaoh. Excellent for expanding the chest and easing tasks.

dhikirs.arabicText: رَبَّنَا آتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
dhikirs.transliteration: Rabbana atina fid-dunya hasanatan wa fil-akhirati hasanatan wa qina 'adhaban-nar
dhikirs.englishTranslation: Our Lord, give us good in this world and good in the Hereafter, and protect us from the punishment of the Fire
dhikirs.benefit: One of the most comprehensive duas in the Quran. The Prophet (SAW) used to make this dua frequently.

dhikirs.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ الْهُدَى وَالتُّقَى وَالْعَفَافَ وَالْغِنَى
dhikirs.transliteration: Allahumma inni as'alukal-huda wat-tuqa wal-'afafa wal-ghina
dhikirs.englishTranslation: O Allah, I ask You for guidance, piety, chastity, and self-sufficiency
dhikirs.benefit: A beautiful comprehensive dua asking Allah for the essentials of a good life.

dhikirs.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ اهْدِنِي وَسَدِّدْنِي
dhikirs.transliteration: Allahumma-hdini wa saddidni
dhikirs.englishTranslation: O Allah, guide me and keep me on the right path
dhikirs.benefit: A concise dua for guidance. The Prophet (SAW) told Ali (RA) to remember by 'guidance' the guidance of the path.

dhikirs.arabicText: أَصْبَحْنَا وَأَصْبَحَ الْمُلْكُ لِلَّهِ، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
dhikirs.transliteration: Asbahna wa asbahal-mulku lillah, wal-hamdulillah, la ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lah, lahul-mulku wa lahul-hamdu wa huwa 'ala kulli shay'in qadir
dhikirs.englishTranslation: We have entered the morning and the dominion belongs to Allah. All praise is due to Allah. None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, without partner. To Him belongs the dominion and to Him belongs all praise, and He is over all things capable
dhikirs.benefit: One of the morning adhkar. Establishes tawheed and gratitude at the start of each day.

dhikirs.arabicText: أَمْسَيْنَا وَأَمْسَى الْمُلْكُ لِلَّهِ، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
dhikirs.transliteration: Amsayna wa amsal-mulku lillah, wal-hamdulillah, la ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lah, lahul-mulku wa lahul-hamdu wa huwa 'ala kulli shay'in qadir
dhikirs.englishTranslation: We have entered the evening and the dominion belongs to Allah. All praise is due to Allah. None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, without partner. To Him belongs the dominion and to Him belongs all praise, and He is over all things capable
dhikirs.benefit: The evening version of the morning dhikir. Said in the evening to acknowledge Allah's sovereignty.

dhikirs.arabicText: بِاسْمِكَ اللَّهُمَّ أَمُوتُ وَأَحْيَا
dhikirs.transliteration: Bismika Allahumma amutu wa ahya
dhikirs.englishTranslation: In Your name, O Allah, I die and I live
dhikirs.benefit: The Prophet (SAW) would say this when going to sleep, acknowledging that sleep is a minor death.

dhikirs.arabicText: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي أَحْيَانَا بَعْدَ مَا أَمَاتَنَا وَإِلَيْهِ النُّشُورُ
dhikirs.transliteration: Alhamdulillahil-ladhi ahyana ba'da ma amatana wa ilayhin-nushur
dhikirs.englishTranslation: All praise is due to Allah Who has given us life after causing us to die, and to Him is the resurrection
dhikirs.benefit: Said immediately upon waking. Acknowledging Allah's power over life and death.

dhikirs.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ عَافِنِي فِي بَدَنِي، اللَّهُمَّ عَافِنِي فِي سَمْعِي، اللَّهُمَّ عَافِنِي فِي بَصَرِي، لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ
dhikirs.transliteration: Allahumma 'afini fi badani, Allahumma 'afini fi sam'i, Allahumma 'afini fi basari, la ilaha illa ant
dhikirs.englishTranslation: O Allah, grant me health in my body. O Allah, grant me health in my hearing. O Allah, grant me health in my sight. None has the right to be worshipped except You
dhikirs.benefit: A morning dua asking Allah for health in body, hearing, and sight - our most important faculties.

dhikirs.arabicText: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الَّذِي لَا يَضُرُّ مَعَ اسْمِهِ شَيْءٌ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِي السَّمَاءِ وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
dhikirs.transliteration: Bismillahil-ladhi la yadurru ma'asmihi shay'un fil-ardi wa la fis-sama'i wa huwas-Sami'ul-'Alim
dhikirs.englishTranslation: In the name of Allah, with whose name nothing can harm on earth or in heaven, and He is the All-Hearing, All-Knowing
dhikirs.benefit: Whoever says this three times in morning and evening, nothing will harm them.

dhikirs.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ الْعَفْوَ وَالْعَافِيَةَ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَالْآخِرَةِ
dhikirs.transliteration: Allahumma inni as'alukal-'afwa wal-'afiyata fid-dunya wal-akhirah
dhikirs.englishTranslation: O Allah, I ask You for pardon and well-being in this world and the Hereafter
dhikirs.benefit: The Prophet (SAW) said: 'Ask Allah for pardon and well-being, for after certainty of faith, no one is given anything better than well-being.'

dhikirs.arabicText: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ
dhikirs.transliteration: Subhanallahi wa bihamdihi
dhikirs.englishTranslation: Glory be to Allah and praise Him
dhikirs.benefit: Whoever says this 100 times in the morning and evening will have his sins forgiven even if they were like the foam of the sea.

dhikirs.arabicText: لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
dhikirs.transliteration: La ilaha illallahu wahdahu la sharika lah, lahul-mulku wa lahul-hamdu wa huwa 'ala kulli shay'in qadir
dhikirs.englishTranslation: None has the right to be worshipped except Allah, alone, without partner. To Him belongs the dominion and to Him belongs all praise, and He is over all things capable
dhikirs.benefit: Whoever says this 10 times will have the reward of freeing four slaves from the children of Isma'il.

dhikirs.arabicText: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ، وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ، وَلَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ، وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ
dhikirs.transliteration: Subhanallah, wal-hamdulillah, wa la ilaha illallah, wallahu akbar
dhikirs.englishTranslation: Glory be to Allah, all praise is due to Allah, none has the right to be worshipped except Allah, and Allah is the Greatest
dhikirs.benefit: The four beloved phrases to Allah. More beloved to the Prophet (SAW) than everything the sun rises upon.

dhikirs.arabicText: أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ
dhikirs.transliteration: Astaghfirullah
dhikirs.englishTranslation: I seek forgiveness from Allah
dhikirs.benefit: The Prophet (SAW) used to seek forgiveness from Allah more than 70 times a day.

dhikirs.arabicText: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ الْعَظِيمِ
dhikirs.transliteration: Subhanallahi wa bihamdihi, subhanallahil-'Azim
dhikirs.englishTranslation: Glory be to Allah and praise Him, glory be to Allah the Almighty
dhikirs.benefit: Two words light on the tongue, heavy on the scales, beloved to the Most Merciful.

dhikirs.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَعَلَى آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ كَمَا صَلَّيْتَ عَلَى إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَعَلَى آلِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ إِنَّكَ حَمِيدٌ مَجِيدٌ
dhikirs.transliteration: Allahumma salli 'ala Muhammadin wa 'ala ali Muhammadin kama sallayta 'ala Ibrahima wa 'ala ali Ibrahima innaka Hamidun Majid
dhikirs.englishTranslation: O Allah, send blessings upon Muhammad and upon the family of Muhammad, as You sent blessings upon Ibrahim and the family of Ibrahim. You are Praiseworthy, Glorious
dhikirs.benefit: Sending blessings upon the Prophet (SAW). Whoever sends one blessing, Allah sends ten upon them.

dhikirs.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي ظَلَمْتُ نَفْسِي ظُلْمًا كَثِيرًا وَلَا يَغْفِرُ الذُّنُوبَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ، فَاغْفِرْ لِي مَغْفِرَةً مِنْ عِنْدِكَ وَارْحَمْنِي إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ
dhikirs.transliteration: Allahumma inni zalamtu nafsi zulman kathiran wa la yaghfirudh-dhunuba illa anta, faghfir li maghfiratan min 'indika warhamni innaka antal-Ghafurur-Rahim
dhikirs.englishTranslation: O Allah, I have wronged myself greatly and none forgives sins except You. Grant me forgiveness from Yourself and have mercy on me. You are the Forgiving, the Merciful
dhikirs.benefit: The Prophet (SAW) taught Abu Bakr (RA) to say this in his prayer. A comprehensive dua for forgiveness.

dhikirs.arabicText: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ، تَوَكَّلْتُ عَلَى اللَّهِ، لَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ
dhikirs.transliteration: Bismillah, tawakkaltu 'alallah, la hawla wa la quwwata illa billah
dhikirs.englishTranslation: In the name of Allah, I place my trust in Allah. There is no power or strength except with Allah
dhikirs.benefit: Said when leaving the house. One who says this is guided, protected, and defended, and Satan will stay away.

dhikirs.arabicText: سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي سَخَّرَ لَنَا هَذَا وَمَا كُنَّا لَهُ مُقْرِنِينَ وَإِنَّا إِلَى رَبِّنَا لَمُنْقَلِبُونَ
dhikirs.transliteration: Subhanal-ladhi sakhkhara lana hadha wa ma kunna lahu muqrinin, wa inna ila Rabbina lamunqalibun
dhikirs.englishTranslation: Glory be to Him Who has subjected this to us, and we could never have it by our efforts. And to our Lord we shall return
dhikirs.benefit: The Quranic dua for traveling. Acknowledging that all means of transportation are from Allah.

dhikirs.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّا نَسْأَلُكَ فِي سَفَرِنَا هَذَا الْبِرَّ وَالتَّقْوَى، وَمِنَ الْعَمَلِ مَا تَرْضَى
dhikirs.transliteration: Allahumma inna nas'aluka fi safarina hadhal-birra wat-taqwa, wa minal-'amali ma tarda
dhikirs.englishTranslation: O Allah, we ask You in this journey of ours for righteousness, piety, and deeds that please You
dhikirs.benefit: Part of the comprehensive travel supplication taught by the Prophet (SAW).

dhikirs.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ اكْفِنِيهِمْ بِمَا شِئْتَ
dhikirs.transliteration: Allahummak-finihim bima shi't
dhikirs.englishTranslation: O Allah, suffice me against them however You will
dhikirs.benefit: Said when fearing harm from others. Entrusting one's affairs entirely to Allah.

dhikirs.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ رَبَّ النَّاسِ أَذْهِبِ الْبَاسَ، اشْفِ أَنْتَ الشَّافِي، لَا شِفَاءَ إِلَّا شِفَاؤُكَ، شِفَاءً لَا يُغَادِرُ سَقَمًا
dhikirs.transliteration: Allahumma Rabban-nas, adhhibil-ba's, ishfi antash-Shafi, la shifa'a illa shifa'uk, shifa'an la yughadiru saqama
dhikirs.englishTranslation: O Allah, Lord of mankind, remove the hardship. Heal, for You are the Healer. There is no healing except Your healing, a healing that leaves no illness behind
dhikirs.benefit: The Prophet (SAW) would visit the sick and make this dua for them. A powerful supplication for healing.

dhikirs.arabicText: بِسْمِ اللَّهِ أَرْقِيكَ، مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ يُؤْذِيكَ، مِنْ شَرِّ كُلِّ نَفْسٍ أَوْ عَيْنِ حَاسِدٍ، اللَّهُ يَشْفِيكَ، بِسْمِ اللَّهِ أَرْقِيكَ
dhikirs.transliteration: Bismillahi arqik, min kulli shay'in yu'dhik, min sharri kulli nafsin aw 'aynin hasid, Allahu yashfik, bismillahi arqik
dhikirs.englishTranslation: In the name of Allah I perform ruqyah for you, from everything that harms you, from the evil of every soul or envious eye. May Allah heal you. In the name of Allah I perform ruqyah for you
dhikirs.benefit: The ruqyah (spiritual healing) that Jibreel performed on the Prophet (SAW).

dhikirs.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْتَخِيرُكَ بِعِلْمِكَ، وَأَسْتَقْدِرُكَ بِقُدْرَتِكَ، وَأَسْأَلُكَ مِنْ فَضْلِكَ الْعَظِيمِ
dhikirs.transliteration: Allahumma inni astakhiruka bi'ilmik, wa astaqdiruka biqudratik, wa as'aluka min fadlikal-'azim
dhikirs.englishTranslation: O Allah, I seek Your guidance by virtue of Your knowledge, and I seek ability by virtue of Your power, and I ask You of Your great bounty
dhikirs.benefit: The beginning of Salat al-Istikhara. Seeking Allah's guidance before making important decisions.

dhikirs.arabicText: رَبِّ لَا تَذَرْنِي فَرْدًا وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ الْوَارِثِينَ
dhikirs.transliteration: Rabbi la tadhirni fardan wa anta khayrul-warithin
dhikirs.englishTranslation: My Lord, do not leave me alone, and You are the best of inheritors
dhikirs.benefit: The dua of Prophet Zakariya (AS). Excellent for those feeling alone or longing for companionship.

dhikirs.arabicText: وَقُل رَّبِّ زِدْنِي عِلْمًا
dhikirs.transliteration: Wa qul Rabbi zidni 'ilma
dhikirs.englishTranslation: And say: My Lord, increase me in knowledge
dhikirs.benefit: Allah commanded the Prophet (SAW) to ask for increase only in knowledge. Knowledge is the greatest treasure.

dhikirs.arabicText: رَبِّ أَوْزِعْنِي أَنْ أَشْكُرَ نِعْمَتَكَ الَّتِي أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيَّ وَعَلَىٰ وَالِدَيَّ وَأَنْ أَعْمَلَ صَالِحًا تَرْضَاهُ
dhikirs.transliteration: Rabbi awzi'ni an ashkura ni'matakalati an'amta 'alayya wa 'ala walidayya wa an a'mala salihan tardah
dhikirs.englishTranslation: My Lord, enable me to be grateful for Your favor which You have bestowed upon me and upon my parents and to do righteousness of which You approve
dhikirs.benefit: The dua of Prophet Sulayman (AS). Asking for the ability to be truly grateful.

dhikirs.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ أَلْهِمْنِي رُشْدِي، وَأَعِذْنِي مِنْ شَرِّ نَفْسِي
dhikirs.transliteration: Allahumma alhimni rushdi, wa a'idhni min sharri nafsi
dhikirs.englishTranslation: O Allah, inspire me with guidance and protect me from the evil of my own self
dhikirs.benefit: A beautiful dua asking for inspiration and protection from one's own nafs (self).

dhikirs.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْكَسَلِ وَالْهَرَمِ، وَالْمَأْثَمِ وَالْمَغْرَمِ
dhikirs.transliteration: Allahumma inni a'udhu bika minal-kasali wal-haram, wal-ma'thami wal-maghram
dhikirs.englishTranslation: O Allah, I seek refuge in You from laziness and old age, from sin and debt
dhikirs.benefit: The Prophet (SAW) frequently sought refuge from these four things that weaken a person.

dhikirs.arabicText: لَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ
dhikirs.transliteration: La hawla wa la quwwata illa billah
dhikirs.englishTranslation: There is no power or strength except with Allah
dhikirs.benefit: One of the treasures from beneath the Throne of Allah. Said when feeling powerless or overwhelmed.

dhikirs.arabicText: سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ الْعَظِيمِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ
dhikirs.transliteration: Subhanallahil-'Azimi wa bihamdih
dhikirs.englishTranslation: Glory be to Allah the Almighty and praise Him
dhikirs.benefit: Whoever says this, a palm tree is planted for them in Paradise.

dhikirs.arabicText: يَا مُقَلِّبَ الْقُلُوبِ ثَبِّتْ قَلْبِي عَلَى دِينِكَ
dhikirs.transliteration: Ya Muqallibal-qulub, thabbit qalbi 'ala dinik
dhikirs.englishTranslation: O Turner of hearts, make my heart firm upon Your religion
dhikirs.benefit: The Prophet (SAW) made this dua frequently. Our hearts are between Allah's fingers, and He turns them as He wills.

dhikirs.arabicText: رَبَّنَا لَا تُزِغْ قُلُوبَنَا بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدَيْتَنَا وَهَبْ لَنَا مِن لَّدُنكَ رَحْمَةً إِنَّكَ أَنتَ الْوَهَّابُ
dhikirs.transliteration: Rabbana la tuzigh qulubana ba'da idh hadaytana wa hab lana min ladunka rahmah, innaka antal-Wahhab
dhikirs.englishTranslation: Our Lord, do not let our hearts deviate after You have guided us, and grant us mercy from Yourself. Indeed, You are the Bestower
dhikirs.benefit: A Quranic dua asking Allah to keep our hearts steadfast on guidance.

dhikirs.arabicText: رَبَّنَا أَفْرِغْ عَلَيْنَا صَبْرًا وَثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَنَا وَانصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ
dhikirs.transliteration: Rabbana afrigh 'alayna sabran wa thabbit aqdamana wansurna 'alal-qawmil-kafirin
dhikirs.englishTranslation: Our Lord, pour upon us patience and plant firmly our feet and give us victory over the disbelieving people
dhikirs.benefit: The dua of the believers when facing Goliath. Asking for patience and steadfastness.

dhikirs.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ اجْعَلْنِي شَكُورًا، وَاجْعَلْنِي صَبُورًا، وَاجْعَلْنِي فِي عَيْنِي صَغِيرًا، وَفِي أَعْيُنِ النَّاسِ كَبِيرًا
dhikirs.transliteration: Allahummaj'alni shakura, waj'alni sabura, waj'alni fi 'ayni saghira, wa fi a'yunin-nasi kabira
dhikirs.englishTranslation: O Allah, make me grateful, make me patient, make me small in my own eyes and great in the eyes of people
dhikirs.benefit: A comprehensive dua asking for gratitude, patience, humility, and honor.

dhikirs.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ زَوَالِ نِعْمَتِكَ، وَتَحَوُّلِ عَافِيَتِكَ، وَفُجَاءَةِ نِقْمَتِكَ، وَجَمِيعِ سَخَطِكَ
dhikirs.transliteration: Allahumma inni a'udhu bika min zawali ni'matik, wa tahawwuli 'afiyatik, wa fuja'ati niqmatik, wa jami'i sakhatik
dhikirs.englishTranslation: O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the withdrawal of Your blessing, the change of Your protection, Your sudden punishment, and all Your displeasure
dhikirs.benefit: A comprehensive dua seeking protection from losing Allah's favors and blessings.

dhikirs.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْأَلُكَ عِلْمًا نَافِعًا، وَرِزْقًا طَيِّبًا، وَعَمَلًا مُتَقَبَّلًا
dhikirs.transliteration: Allahumma inni as'aluka 'ilman nafi'a, wa rizqan tayyiba, wa 'amalan mutaqabbala
dhikirs.englishTranslation: O Allah, I ask You for beneficial knowledge, good provision, and accepted deeds
dhikirs.benefit: The Prophet (SAW) would say this after Fajr prayer. Asking for the three pillars of a successful life.

dhikirs.arabicText: رَضِيتُ بِاللَّهِ رَبًّا، وَبِالْإِسْلَامِ دِينًا، وَبِمُحَمَّدٍ نَبِيًّا
dhikirs.transliteration: Raditu billahi Rabba, wa bil-Islami dina, wa bi-Muhammadin nabiyya
dhikirs.englishTranslation: I am pleased with Allah as Lord, Islam as religion, and Muhammad as Prophet
dhikirs.benefit: Whoever says this in morning and evening, it becomes a right upon Allah to please them on the Day of Judgment.

dhikirs.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ مَا أَصْبَحَ بِي مِنْ نِعْمَةٍ أَوْ بِأَحَدٍ مِنْ خَلْقِكَ فَمِنْكَ وَحْدَكَ لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ، فَلَكَ الْحَمْدُ وَلَكَ الشُّكْرُ
dhikirs.transliteration: Allahumma ma asbaha bi min ni'matin aw bi-ahadin min khalqik, faminka wahdaka la sharika lak, falakal-hamdu wa lakash-shukr
dhikirs.englishTranslation: O Allah, whatever blessing I or any of Your creation have risen upon is from You alone, without partner. So for You is all praise and to You is all gratitude
dhikirs.benefit: Whoever says this in the morning has fulfilled the gratitude of that day.

dhikirs.arabicText: سُبْحَانَكَ اللَّهُمَّ وَبِحَمْدِكَ، أَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ، أَسْتَغْفِرُكَ وَأَتُوبُ إِلَيْكَ
dhikirs.transliteration: Subhanakallahumma wa bihamdik, ashhadu an la ilaha illa ant, astaghfiruka wa atubu ilayk
dhikirs.englishTranslation: Glory be to You, O Allah, and praise be to You. I bear witness that there is no deity except You. I seek Your forgiveness and repent to You
dhikirs.benefit: The kaffarat al-majlis (expiation of the gathering). Said at the end of any gathering to expiate any wrong speech.

dhikirs.arabicText: سُبُّوحٌ قُدُّوسٌ رَبُّ الْمَلَائِكَةِ وَالرُّوحِ
dhikirs.transliteration: Subbuhun Quddusun Rabbul-mala'ikati war-Ruh
dhikirs.englishTranslation: Perfect and Holy, Lord of the angels and the Spirit
dhikirs.benefit: The Prophet (SAW) would say this in his ruku' and sujud. A glorification of Allah's perfection.

dhikirs.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ بِكَ أَصْبَحْنَا وَبِكَ أَمْسَيْنَا، وَبِكَ نَحْيَا وَبِكَ نَمُوتُ، وَإِلَيْكَ النُّشُورُ
dhikirs.transliteration: Allahumma bika asbahna wa bika amsayna, wa bika nahya wa bika namutu, wa ilaykan-nushur
dhikirs.englishTranslation: O Allah, by You we enter the morning and by You we enter the evening, by You we live and by You we die, and to You is the resurrection
dhikirs.benefit: A morning and evening dhikir acknowledging Allah's complete control over our lives.

dhikirs.arabicText: أَعُوذُ بِكَلِمَاتِ اللَّهِ التَّامَّاتِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ
dhikirs.transliteration: A'udhu bi kalimatillahit-tammati min sharri ma khalaq
dhikirs.englishTranslation: I seek refuge in the perfect words of Allah from the evil of what He has created
dhikirs.benefit: The Prophet (SAW) said whoever says this upon stopping somewhere, nothing will harm them until they leave.

dhikirs.arabicText: اللَّهُمَّ أَنْتَ رَبِّي لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا أَنْتَ، خَلَقْتَنِي وَأَنَا عَبْدُكَ، وَأَنَا عَلَى عَهْدِكَ وَوَعْدِكَ مَا اسْتَطَعْتُ
dhikirs.transliteration: Allahumma anta Rabbi la ilaha illa ant, khalaqtani wa ana 'abduk, wa ana 'ala 'ahdika wa wa'dika mastata't
dhikirs.englishTranslation: O Allah, You are my Lord, none has the right to be worshipped except You. You created me and I am Your servant, and I abide to Your covenant and promise as best I can
dhikirs.benefit: Sayyid al-Istighfar (Master of Seeking Forgiveness). Whoever says it in the morning with conviction and dies that day enters Paradise.
